message:
feat(abi): expose 16 new BSD/POSIX/BIND symbols + harden stdlib_abi env path

Caps a wave of recently-shipped per-symbol ports by adding their
public exports to the version script and tightening the surrounding
environ/explicit_bzero machinery. After this commit total_exported
moves from 3980 to 3996 symbols; all 16 new entries are
Implemented (no stubs) with both strict and hardened semantics
recorded.

Version-script exports added (crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map):

  string family          strmode, strnstr,
                         timingsafe_bcmp, timingsafe_memcmp
  stdlib                 recallocarray, freezero, getbsize
  unistd                 posix_close
  err.h explicit-code    errc, warnc, verrc, vwarnc
                         (registered in both the BSD-cluster and
                          err.h-cluster blocks; nodes match the
                          existing err/warn/verr/vwarn placement)
  resolver / BIND        __b64_ntop, __b64_pton, b64_ntop, b64_pton

Each symbol's underlying implementation already landed in prior
commits (timingsafe in 0b528929, strmode in ae225490, warnc/verrc
family in 39d02be1, strnstr in 7e92d96c, freezero/recallocarray in
e41feee4, posix_close in 48263c3c, b64 in 450d6f4c, getbsize in the
in-flight bd-ts3l3 work). The version script is the public-ABI
gate: linking against libfrankenlibc_abi.so was previously not
finding these symbols even though the Rust code existed. This
commit makes them externally visible.

stdlib_abi.rs hardening (~275 lines net):

- New helpers:
  - set_abi_errno_if_clear(c_int): sets errno only if the slot
    currently reads 0, so ABI paths that already wrote a more
    specific errno are not clobbered by a fall-through default.
  - bounded_zero_len(ptr, requested) -> Option<usize>: clamps the
    explicit_bzero / freezero zero-fill length to known_remaining()
    of the underlying allocation, then bounds the total at
    isize::MAX (avoids fall-through into UB regions even when the
    caller hands in larger lengths).
  - MAX_EXPLICIT_BZERO_LEN = isize::MAX as usize
- ensure_environ_owned() now returns bool — true iff environ is in
  our allocation. Failure paths (OOM or count*ptr_size overflow on
  count.checked_add(9).checked_mul(ptr_size)) return false instead
  of leaving the environ pointer in an inconsistent half-owned
  state. Callers (take_environ_ownership, native_setenv) handle the
  bool explicitly.

setjmp_abi.rs and runtime_policy.rs test isolation:

- runtime_policy.rs adds a #[cfg(test)] OnceLock<ReentrantMutex<()>>
  via parking_lot, plus a runtime_policy_test_lock() helper that
  serializes any test which touches the global policy state. This
  fixes ordering races between the two longjmp_entrypoint /
  siglongjmp_entrypoint tests when they ran on the same Cargo test
  thread.
- setjmp_abi.rs:
  - Both longjmp tests now hold the runtime-policy test lock for
    their lifetime.
  - capture_entrypoint(...) return value is now asserted equal to
    0 instead of being ignored, catching capture-path regressions
    that would otherwise be invisible to the test.

Other touchpoints in this commit:

- glibc_internal_abi.rs (~148): __b64_ntop / __b64_pton ABI shims +
  surrounding NUL/length contracts, matching the BIND/libresolv
  (src, srclength, target, targsize) and (src, target, targsize)
  signatures.
- malloc_abi.rs (~34): minor surface adjustments for
  freezero/recallocarray hand-off; no behavior change to existing
  malloc semantics.
- core/resolv/b64.rs (~32): pure-byte b64 module touch-ups so the
  ABI shim can hand off via Option<usize>.
- core/stdlib/mod.rs (+1): pub mod getbsize.
- frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs (+79): execute_getbsize_case()
  added to the fixture dispatcher; uses a getbsize_fixture_env_lock
  (OnceLock<Mutex<()>>) to serialize BLOCKSIZE env mutation across
  parallel fixture runs.
- harness/tests/stdlib_numeric_conformance_test.rs (+54): new
  fixture cases exercising getbsize across BLOCKSIZE = unset, 1024,
  "1k", "1m", and bogus inputs.
- harness/tests/claim_reconciliation_test.rs: 3980 -> 3996 in the
  README mutation fixture so the drift detector keeps matching the
  current snapshot string.
- abi/lib.rs: drops the redundant #[unsafe(no_mangle)] on the
  FRANKENLIBC_ABI_INIT_ARRAY .init_array slot — the symbol does not
  need to be globally visible, only the link_section is required.
- abi/tests/{conformance_diff_realpath_tty,glibc_internal_abi,
  stdlib_abi}_test.rs (+341 net): new test cases exercising the
  new symbols and the bounded_zero_len / set_abi_errno_if_clear
  helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
